Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses To Requests For Production (Set One)
Off Calendar Dec-04-2024 Continued To Dec-12-24 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 301

MASTER CALENDAR COURT
Off Calendar Jul-03-2024 Continued To Apr-28-25 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

APPLICATION AND HEARING FOR RIGHT TO ATTACH ORDER
Off Calendar Apr-12-2024 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances :
Atty. MEHA RAJA, Esq. of Niesar & Vestal LLP (tel: 415-882-5300) for Plaintiffs.
Atty. KRISTIN MURPHY, Esq. and CAITLYN BROCK, Esq. of Latham Watkins LLP (tel: 714-540-1235) for Defendant (via Zoom videoconference).

Hearing Re: PLAINTIFF DAVID HALL, MARTA HALL's APPLICATION AND HEARING FOR RIGHT TO ATTACH ORDER.

Matter argued and submitted. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Plaintiffs' application for a writ of attachment is denied. Per CCP 483.010(a), plaintiffs must show that "the total amount of the claim or claims is a fixed or readily ascertainable amount." Plaintiffs fail to meet that burden. Under the parties Bylaws and indemnification agreement, plaintiffs are entitled to be reimbursed for the fees "actually and reasonably incurred." (M. Hall Decl. Ex. 2 at Art. VII, Sec. 7.2; id., Ex. 1 at 2, Sec. 1(b) and 13(e). Here, there is an obvious disagreement regarding what fees plaintiffs reasonably incurred in defending the derivative action. In fact, there is evidence that plaintiffs are seeking to recover fees unrelated to defending that action. (See M. Hall Decl., par. 12, Ex. 6, at HAB001[consideration of offensive claims].)

Order signed.

Judge: Rochelle C. East; Clerk: V. Da Fonseca; Court reporter: Sheila Pham, CSR# 13293 (tel: 415-274-9977/ e-mail: calendar-LA@veritax.com). (302/RCE)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Dec-28-2023 Set For Trial On Sep-09-24 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206
